title: TaxSorted Window Tax media release
rightsBoundary: This manifest records the rights and integrity of the deployed image files. It does not turn a reusable image into proof of every claim made about it.

materials.modifications: Rotated 270 degrees clockwise and resized to a 1600-pixel longest edge at JPEG quality 75; no crop or tonal adjustment.
materials.supports: A contemporary satirical argument that repeal would restore daylight.
materials.doesNotProve: The prevalence of dark housing, a quantified health effect or a real household.

materials.credit: Crown copyright, National Records of Scotland, E326/1/32, page 73.

rights.label: National Records of Scotland copyright and reuse conditions

materials.modifications: No changes; the page-exposed source rendition is stored byte-for-byte.
materials.supports: Specific assessed properties had fewer open windows than at the previous survey.
materials.doesNotProve: Why every opening was closed, how common closure was or the cause of every modern blocked window.

materials.credit: 3 Edgar Street, Worcester by Stephen Richards, CC BY-SA 2.0.
materials.modifications: No changes; the Wikimedia and Geograph source bytes are identical.
materials.supports: The present facade and Historic England's qualified interpretation of a probable tax-related alteration.
materials.doesNotProve: The owner's recorded intention, the date of the alteration or the cause of similar changes elsewhere.
